41b29689be4c.title: Below local median — competitive risk
41b29689be4c.description: Ranked #6 of 9 locally (P37.5). Peers are outperforming you on aggregate.

84abd7b5e537.title: Guest experience trailing local competitors
84abd7b5e537.description: Peers are delivering a more consistent or higher-quality guest experience. Identify what they do differently — is it menu depth, service style, or ambience? — and decide whether to match or differentiate.

356f5b88fba0.title: Compliance record trailing local standards
356f5b88fba0.description: Most local peers carry stronger formal compliance signals. This may not affect daily footfall yet but creates exposure if an inspection goes poorly or a customer complaint escalates.

a5bde007590a.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
a5bde007590a.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
a5bde007590a.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

aea7b90c03a5.title: Address recurring food quality complaints from guests
aea7b90c03a5.description: Food Quality draws recurring criticism across reviews (3 negative mentions). One guest wrote: "I ordered a prawn cocktail which came with bread and butter..." Investigate whether this reflects a systemic issue or isolated incidents.
aea7b90c03a5.expected_upside: Reduce food quality complaints, protect rating
aea7b90c03a5.management_implication: Guests are telling you what's wrong. Listen.

84416e2245d6.title: Address repeated atmosphere & setting complaints from guests
84416e2245d6.description: Atmosphere & Setting draws repeated criticism across reviews (2 negative mentions). One guest wrote: "We waited 40 minutes to eat and only after our protests they brought us the basket of bread ordered ..." Investigate whether this reflects a systemic issue or isolated incidents.
84416e2245d6.expected_upside: Reduce atmosphere & setting complaints, protect rating
84416e2245d6.management_implication: Guests are telling you what's wrong. Listen.

64f68aaa1b94.title: Lean into service & hospitality — it's what guests are buying
64f68aaa1b94.description: Guests consistently praise service & hospitality (18 positive mentions). This appears to be what the venue actually sells, whether or not it's what you think you sell. Make it the headline of your online presence.
64f68aaa1b94.expected_upside: Sharpen proposition → attract the right guests
64f68aaa1b94.management_implication: Your guests are telling you your proposition. Use it.

502d70031501.title: Compliance record weakest in peer set — creates inspection exposure
502d70031501.description: You trail peers by 1.1 points on trust. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.

2413e82c158a.title: Protect the 4.6/5 rating — it took years to build
2413e82c158a.description: With 887 reviews at 4.6/5, your discovery advantage is substantial. Protect it by responding to every review within 48 hours and prompting satisfied tables for feedback.

d5b1c96d26a0.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
d5b1c96d26a0.description: Your 4.6/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P37.5). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
d5b1c96d26a0.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

7385e7daa24d.title: Ranked #6 of 9 locally — peers are ahead
7385e7daa24d.description: At percentile 37.5, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.
